commit.message:
Fix bot 409 conflict: retry with backoff instead of crashing

When Telegram returns 409 (another getUpdates request), wait
5-30s and retry instead of crashing the process, which caused
Docker to instantly restart and repeat the conflict loop.

- Hero: category-owning headline, product-first subheading

- Comparison/HowItWorks/Stats: conviction-driven copy
- Blockchain page: "In code we trust" headline, tighter section copy
- Pricing: two-card layout (Studio free + Trading 6%), dropped hero header
- CTA: cleaner close without fear-based marketing

compact insights layout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: per-audit browser sessions + graceful browser drop handling

- browserbase.ts: replace module-level singletons with Map<auditId, session>
  so concurrent audits each get their own isolated browser session
- supervisor.ts: pass auditId to all browserbase functions
- supervisor.ts: wrap getNewBrowserPage() in try/catch — if browser drops
  after crawl, technical/geo degrade gracefully instead of crashing the
  whole audit (content + schema still run from DB)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix sandbox --copy permissions for container's node user

The temp dir from mktemp has 700 permissions owned by the host user,
making it unreadable by the container's node user (uid 1000).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add /publish admin endpoint (no-op, site is instant)

In the current architecture save IS publish — DB updates are live
immediately. This endpoint exists for the admin toolbar button.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: align code to actual DB schema — chip_transactions.transaction_type + table_waitlists

CRITICAL: After querying the actual Supabase DB, discovered code was using
wrong column names and table names that don't exist:

1. chip_transactions uses 'transaction_type' NOT 'type' — fixed in
   HeadlessTableEngine, TournamentEngine, TableService, AgentService

2. Table is 'table_waitlists' (plural) NOT 'table_waitlist' — fixed in
   TableService, WaitlistService, WaitlistManager components (23 refs)

3. Removed 'reference_id' from chip_transactions inserts (column doesn't exist)

Also applied via Supabase SQL Editor:
- Added status, leave_pending, auto_rebuy columns to table_seats
- Created table_activity table for leave/join logging
- Created fn_add_chips RPC for atomic chip credits
- Created deduct_chip_balance RPC for atomic chip deductions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: restore predictions by defaulting bettable_only to false and persisting line_vendor

bettable_only=true was filtering out all predictions since most lines come
from Balldontlie (vendor 'rebet'), not DraftKings/FanDuel. Also adds
line_source and line_vendor to the orchestrator's PostgreSQL INSERT so
vendor data is preserved for future filtering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

security: restrict HTTPS to Tailscale network only

- docker-compose: bind port 443 to Tailscale IP 100.100.130.77 only
  (port 80 stays public for certbot http-01 ACME renewal)
- nginx: add allow 100.64.0.0/10; deny all; to HTTPS server block
  as defense-in-depth
- ufw: allow port 443 in on tailscale0 interface

Only devices on the Tailscale network can reach sentinelai.cloud.
Cert renewal still works via public port 80 (next renewal ~May 2026).

fix: extract divider color swatches out of innerHTML assignment

Inline Object.keys().map() inside a modal.innerHTML string concatenation
was silently aborting the whole assignment, leaving the modal blank.
Pre-compute the swatch HTML into a variable first, then reference it.

Switch storage to Supabase, drop googleapis

Replace Google Sheets service account with Supabase (postgres).
Prayer logs stored in prayer_logs table, scoped by user email.
Simplified auth — no extra OAuth scopes needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: persist language preference to DB for webhook/edge functions

Root cause: language was only stored in localStorage, so the ManyChat
webhook had no way to know the user's language preference and always
sent 'pt' hardcoded. Now:
- Added `language` column to user_settings table
- LanguageContext syncs language to DB on change and reads from DB on mount
- manychat-webhook reads user language from user_settings
- Updated Supabase types

commit.message:
feat: Session 401 — 10 new data source scrapers, processors, signals, referee fix, CLV tracking

New scrapers: NumberFire, FantasyPros, DailyFantasyFuel, Dimers (projections),
TeamRankings, HashtagBasketball DvP, RotoWire lineups, Covers referee,
NBA tracking stats, VSiN betting splits (external).

10 Phase 2 processors with path extractors and PROCESSOR_REGISTRY entries.
8 new signals: projection_consensus (over/under/disagreement), predicted_pace,
dvp_favorable, closing_line_value (positive/negative CLV).
Fixed broken referee processor (missing load_data method since December).
Added CLV tracking via snapshot_type column on odds_api_player_points_props.
10 BQ tables created. 11 Cloud Scheduler jobs active.

commit.message:
feat(euclid): geometry-aligned citation labels with real-time preview

- CitationFlash is now a discriminated union (segment/circle/extend/point)
  storing geometry so labels align with the construction element
- Segment/extend labels rotate parallel to the line (never upside-down),
  offset to the working-edge side opposite the straightedge bar
- Circle labels curve along the circumference using per-character arc
  placement, with flat fallback for small circles
- Real-time preview labels at 50% alpha during tool use: Post.1 along
  straightedge preview, Post.2 along extend preview, Post.3 on compass arc
- Point placement flashes Def.1, macro application flashes the proposition
- Added Def.1 ("A point is that which has no part") to citations registry

commit.message:
Initial commit

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(assessors): support project-named directories and test-only repos in standard_layout check (#322)

* fix(assessors): support project-named directories and test-only repos in standard_layout

Enhances StandardLayoutAssessor to recognize multiple valid Python project
structures instead of rigidly requiring src/:

- Project-named directories (e.g., pandas/pandas/) now pass the check
- Test-only repositories now receive NOT_APPLICABLE instead of failing
- Parses pyproject.toml for project name (PEP 621 and Poetry formats)
- Falls back to detecting any root directory with __init__.py
- Adds blocklist to exclude non-source directories (tests, docs, utils, etc.)
- Updates remediation to present both src/ and flat layout as valid options

Fixes #246
Fixes #305

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(assessors): address PR #322 review feedback

Changes based on code review:

Critical:
- Remove setup.cfg from test_indicators (false positive risk)
- Sort directory iteration for deterministic cross-platform behavior

Significant:
- Limit Strategy 3 (fallback detection) to repos with pyproject.toml
- Use word-boundary regex for test-only repo name detection
- Expand _NON_SOURCE_DIRS blocklist with migrations, config, etc.

Minor:
- Remove redundant has_tests check (caller already verifies)
- Replace empty strings in commands with comment separators

Updated test to expect failure for project-named directory without
pyproject.toml, matching the new stricter detection behavior.

All 16 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(assessors): address PR #322 second review feedback

Fixes based on code review comment #3948171727:

Bug fixes:
- pytest.ini/conftest.py only indicate test-only repos when pyproject.toml
  is absent; mixed projects typically have pyproject.toml so these files
  alone are not reliable test-only indicators
- Remove celery, middleware, alembic from blocklist since these are
  legitimate package names for their respective projects (Celery, etc.)

Design improvement:
- Strategy 3 (heuristic fallback) now returns type "heuristic" and evidence
  shows "— verify" suffix to flag that this is a best-guess match, not an
  exact name match from pyproject.toml

Style cleanup:
- Remove self-referential "PR #322 feedback:" comments; replaced with
  direct rationale where still needed

Added 3 new tests:
- test_pytest_ini_with_pyproject_is_not_test_only
- test_celery_directory_not_blocked
- test_heuristic_match_shows_verify_in_evidence

All 19 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(assessors): address PR #322 third review feedback

High Priority Fixes:
- Add TypedDict (SourceDirectoryInfo) for _find_source_directory return type
  to provide type safety and prevent runtime key typos
- Fix Strategy 3 scoping bug: heuristic fallback now runs whenever
  pyproject.toml exists, not just when a package name is found. This allows
  repos with pyproject.toml containing only [build-system] to still benefit
  from heuristic source directory detection.

Medium Priority (Tests):
- Add test for pyproject.toml without name field (only [build-system])
- Add test for project-named directory without __init__.py (namespace packages)
  verifying Strategy 2 falls through to Strategy 3 correctly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: fix black formatting in structure.py

Add missing blank line after TypedDict class definition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(assessors): externalize non-source dirs blocklist to Python.arsrc

Addresses PR #322 review Comment 1:
- Create Python.arsrc config file with gitignore-like format
- Add _load_arsrc_file() with @lru_cache for efficient config loading
- Add UserWarning when config file is missing (helps detect packaging issues)
- Update pyproject.toml to include *.arsrc in package-data
- Add 7 new tests covering config loading, parsing, and packaging

The blocklist is now externalized following the reviewer's suggestion to
adopt a github/gitignore-like pattern for managing language conventions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Refactor package data entries in pyproject.toml

Remove conflicting entries and standardize package data.

commit.message:
Add 5 new task configs for remaining core-7 side tasks (#12)

Add SideTaskConfig entries for language_dependent_alignment, obscure_model_reasoning,
deployment_context_aware, narrow_expert_humaneval, and password_locked_biology.

Each config includes full character bios (pro + anti), blocklist patterns, elicitation
prompts, contamination topics, and evaluation question files. Evaluation question JSON
files created for all 5 tasks with task-appropriate formats (bilingual for language
alignment, GSM8K-style for obscure reasoning, deployment/evaluation classification,
programming confidence proxy, and biology/math with password conditions).

Generator scripts updated to dynamically accept all registered task IDs.

fix: pre_wp_mail hook and strengthen Rules Engine spam detection

- Fix critical bug: replace wp_mail filter with pre_wp_mail short-circuit
  filter so blocked emails are actually prevented from being sent
- Add adult/sexual content keywords to SPAM_PHRASES
- Add DARKNET_PHRASES check (+0.30) for darknet/underground market content
- Add check_non_latin_script() (+0.40 Cyrillic, +0.30 Arabic/CJK/etc.)
  to block Russian and other non-English spam on US-targeted sites
- Expand SUSPICIOUS_TLDS with .top, .cc, .to, .onion, .su, .at, .biz

commit.message:
feat: opportunistic GPU scheduling - extra worker when backtest phase idle

get_max_parallel() now detects workers in 'backtest'/'done' phase.
Those workers use ~0.5GB VRAM (vs 3.5GB for training), so freed GPU
slots are offered to new training trials: opportunistic_cap = _MAX_PAR_CAP + n_backtest

- Profile stored at ~/.strides_ai/profile.md, created from template on first run
- User edits it freely in any text editor; no structured format to get wrong
- Raw Markdown is injected directly into the system prompt each session
- strides-ai --setup-profile opens the file in $EDITOR for quick edits
- On first run, file is created and opened automatically before starting chat

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Document SSH agent setup before uv sync

The pyfangs dependency is fetched via SSH from a private repo.
Without a loaded SSH key, uv sync hangs silently. Added instructions
to load the key first and a tip to use -v for debugging.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Railway deployment - implement lazy database initialization

- Delay engine creation until first use to prevent SQLAlchemy from importing psycopg2 at module load time
- Replace direct async_session reference with get_session_factory() function
- Update fudo_client to use lazy session factory for credential retrieval
- This resolves the 'ModuleNotFoundError: No module named psycopg2' crash on Railway

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: embedding, reranking api (#99)

* feat: upgrade default embedding and reranking models

Switch embedding model from mixedbread-ai/mxbai-embed-xsmall-v1 to
sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2. Benchmarks show all-MiniLM-L6-v2
(384d) outperforms 768d code-specialized models (GraphCodeBERT, CodeBERT)
on CodeSearchNet, making it the best free open-source option at this
dimension.

Switch reranking model from mixedbread-ai/mxbai-rerank-xsmall-v1 to
BAAI/bge-reranker-base. The bge-reranker-base is MIT licensed, ships a
native onnx/model.onnx compatible with the existing OrtReranking adapter,
and provides meaningfully stronger reranking quality (~110M vs ~22M params).

Update docs to reflect new model names and sizes.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013HNwaLqsr8sQuYTXFiFGDw

* docs: fix remaining model name references in README and docs

Update README.md, docs/architecture/overview.md, and
docs/features/getting-started.md to reflect the new default models
(all-MiniLM-L6-v2 for embedding, bge-reranker-base for reranking).

https://claude.ai/code/session_013HNwaLqsr8sQuYTXFiFGDw

* feat: add LM Studio adapter for embeddings and reranking

Introduces two new adapters that route embedding and reranking calls to
a local LM Studio instance (or any OpenAI-compatible server) instead of
the bundled ONNX models, enabled via --lm-studio-embeddings.

LmStudioEmbedding (src/connector/adapter/lm_studio_embedding.rs):
- POST /v1/embeddings (OpenAI-compatible endpoint)
- Sorts response data by index (spec doesn't guarantee order)
- L2-normalises output vectors (same as OrtEmbedding)
- Logs a warning when the model returns unexpected dimensions
- Config: LM_STUDIO_BASE_URL (falls back to ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL,
  default http://localhost:1234), LM_STUDIO_EMBEDDING_MODEL

LmStudioReranking (src/connector/adapter/lm_studio_reranking.rs):
- Delegates to the existing ChatClient trait (AnthropicClient/LM Studio
  /v1/messages), same endpoint used by query expansion
- Sends query + truncated snippets in one prompt; expects a JSON float
  array of relevance scores in input order
- Falls back to original retrieval scores on any error so search always
  returns results
- Config: ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_MODEL, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY

Container wiring: --lm-studio-embeddings CLI flag (global) selects the
new adapters; existing ONNX path is unchanged.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013HNwaLqsr8sQuYTXFiFGDw

* refactor: replace --lm-studio-embeddings with --embedding-target and enforce namespace config

Replaces the boolean --lm-studio-embeddings flag with a proper enum
--embedding-target=onnx|api (default: onnx) and adds two companion flags:

  --embedding-model=<id>       HuggingFace model ID (onnx) or API model name
  --embedding-dimensions=<n>   Output dimensionality (default 384)

Namespace config persistence (duckdb_vector_repository.rs):
- New namespace_config table stores (namespace, embedding_target,
  embedding_model, dimensions) on first index
- Subsequent opens validate the provided config against the stored one:
  * Dimension mismatch → hard error with re-index instructions
  * Model mismatch     → hard error (different embedding space)
- FLOAT[{dims}] in the embeddings table is now dynamic, created with
  the configured dimension count instead of a hardcoded 384

LmStudioEmbedding cleanup:
- Constructor now takes (model, dimensions) explicitly (no env-var
  defaults for model — user must pass --embedding-model)
- Uses ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL for the endpoint (same variable as the chat
  client), no separate LM_STUDIO_* vars needed

ContainerConfig:
- embedding_target: EmbeddingTarget (Onnx | Api)
- embedding_model: Option<String>
- embedding_dimensions: usize (default 384)
- lm_studio_embeddings: bool removed

https://claude.ai/code/session_013HNwaLqsr8sQuYTXFiFGDw

* docs: add embedding backends doc and update indexing/search docs

- Create docs/features/embedding-backends.md covering ONNX and API
  backends, LM Studio setup, namespace config enforcement, CLI
  reference, and example aliases
- Update indexing.md to remove hardcoded 384-dimension references and
  document both backends in the embedding and reranking sections
- Update search.md to make diagram and pipeline description
  backend-agnostic

https://claude.ai/code/session_013HNwaLqsr8sQuYTXFiFGDw

* feat: add OpenAI adapter and decouple reranking target from embedding

- Replace LmStudioEmbedding with OpenAiEmbedding using OPENAI_BASE_URL
  (default http://localhost:1234); removes the confusing ANTHROPIC_*
  variable for embeddings since Anthropic has no embedding models
- Rename LmStudioReranking to AnthropicReranking (still uses
  /v1/messages and ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL/ANTHROPIC_MODEL/ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
- Add OpenAiReranking using /v1/chat/completions with OPENAI_BASE_URL,
  OPENAI_MODEL, and OPENAI_API_KEY
- Add RerankingTarget enum (onnx | api/anthropic | api/openai) with
  --reranking-target CLI flag; reranking is now fully independent of
  --embedding-target
- Default reranking target remains onnx for backward compatibility

https://claude.ai/code/session_013HNwaLqsr8sQuYTXFiFGDw

* feat: add OpenAI-compatible query expansion and OpenAiChatClient

- Add OpenAiChatClient implementing ChatClient via /v1/chat/completions
  (uses OPENAI_BASE_URL, OPENAI_MODEL, OPENAI_API_KEY)
- Add QueryExpansionTarget enum (anthropic | open-ai) and
  --expand-query-target CLI flag; defaults to anthropic for backward
  compatibility
- Refactor OpenAiReranking to inject Arc<dyn ChatClient> (via
  OpenAiChatClient) rather than embedding its own HTTP client, matching
  the pattern used by AnthropicReranking

https://claude.ai/code/session_013HNwaLqsr8sQuYTXFiFGDw

* feat: add --embedding-requests flag for concurrent embedding during indexing

- Add --embedding-requests <N> CLI flag (default: 4) controlling the
  maximum number of concurrent embed_chunks calls during indexing
- Restructure both index and incremental_index loops to run read/parse/embed
  concurrently via buffer_unordered(N), then drain results into the
  sequential DuckDB writer — eliminates the per-file network round-trip
  stall when using the API embedding target
- Fix API embedding default model to sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2
  (same as ONNX) instead of the opaque placeholder string
- Add embed_concurrency field to ContainerConfig and
  with_embed_concurrency() builder on IndexRepositoryUseCase

For ONNX the gain is bounded by CPU core count; for API targets N=4
typically reduces wall-clock indexing time by ~4x at the cost of N
concurrent HTTP connections to the embedding server.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013HNwaLqsr8sQuYTXFiFGDw

* fix: update duckdb_vector_repository_tests to match current API

- Replace removed DuckdbVectorRepository::new() with in_memory() for
  tests that don't need namespace isolation
- Add NamespaceEmbeddingConfig argument to new_with_namespace() call in
  the namespace-isolation test
- Export NamespaceEmbeddingConfig from lib.rs so tests can construct it
- Add try_in_memory / try_with_namespace helpers that skip gracefully
  when the vss extension cannot be downloaded (network-restricted CI)

https://claude.ai/code/session_013HNwaLqsr8sQuYTXFiFGDw

* fix: address four robustness issues flagged in code review

- openai_chat_client: log warn! with masked key when
  HeaderValue::from_str fails instead of silently dropping the header
- openai_chat_client: make from_env() fallible (Result<Self, reqwest::Error>)
  so client build errors propagate to callers instead of panicking;
  update two call sites in container.rs to use ?
- openai_reranking: replace byte-indexed slice with char_indices().nth()
  to avoid panics on multi-byte UTF-8 content
- main: reject --embedding-dimensions=0 and --embedding-requests=0
  at startup with a clear error message

https://claude.ai/code/session_013HNwaLqsr8sQuYTXFiFGDw

* fix: address four additional robustness issues

- openai_chat_client: log warn! and include error message when
  resp.text() fails reading the error response body, rather than
  silently swallowing the I/O error with unwrap_or_default
- openai_reranking: change OPENAI_MODEL fallback from "openai-reranker"
  to "openai-chat" to match docs and the chat client's own default
- openai_reranking: preserve highlights from original SearchResult when
  rebuilding reranked results; previously SearchResult::new() dropped
  any highlights that upstream search had attached
- container: return a clear Err when --embedding-target=api is used
  without --embedding-model instead of silently falling back to the
  ONNX default model name

https://claude.ai/code/session_013HNwaLqsr8sQuYTXFiFGDw

---------

backend container. Fixes API calls failing in production.
